exercises.title: Variable Assignment and Reassignment
exercises.description: Variables can be reassigned. Trace the value of 'x' through three assignments and print after each one.
exercises.starterCode:
x = 10
print(x)   # TODO: Should print 10

x = x + 5
print(x)   # TODO: Should print 15

x = x * 2
print(x)   # TODO: Should print 30
hints: The right side is evaluated first, then assigned to the left side.
x = x + 5 reads the current value of x, adds 5, then stores the result back in x.

exercises.title: Multiple Assignment
exercises.description: Python allows assigning multiple variables in one line. Use multiple assignment to swap two values without a temporary variable, then print both.
exercises.starterCode:
a = 100
b = 200

print(f"Before: a={a}, b={b}")

# TODO: Swap a and b in one line using Python's multiple assignment
a, b = b, a

print(f"After:  a={a}, b={b}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
Before: a=100, b=200
After:  a=200, b=100
hints: Python evaluates the right side fully before assigning: a, b = b, a
This is called 'tuple unpacking' and is unique to Python.

exercises.title: Augmented Assignment Operators
exercises.description: Use augmented assignment operators (+=, -=, *=, //=) to update a running total through a series of operations.
exercises.starterCode:
# Start with a score of 100
score = 100

# TODO: Add 25 using +=
score += 25
print(f"After += 25: {score}")

# TODO: Subtract 10 using -=
score -= 10
print(f"After -= 10: {score}")

# TODO: Multiply by 2 using *=
score *= 2
print(f"After *= 2: {score}")

# TODO: Floor-divide by 3 using //=
score //= 3
print(f"After //= 3: {score}")
hints: score += 25 is shorthand for score = score + 25
Each operation uses the current value of score.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: What is a Variable?
warmup.description: A variable stores a value and can be updated. What does this print?
warmup.starterCode:
# Predict the output
counter = 0
counter = counter + 1
counter = counter + 1
counter = counter + 1
print(counter)
